is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum isolates carrying wild-type and triple-mutant dhfr alleles from 11 African countries.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Genotyped dhfr SNPs at codons 51, 59, 108 (N51I, C59R, S108N) — all curated Pfdhfr target codons — plus 164.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives counts of WT/3M/4M dhfr alleles per country/location (e.g. Gabon Bakoumba WT 10, 3M 40).

sub_country_location.evidence: Table 1 gives sub-country locations for field-collected samples (Bakoumba, Kolle, Bougoula-Hameau, Pointe-Noire/Brazzaville, Hohoe/Navrongo, Kasangati), but a large fraction (173 isolates) are imported traveller cases diagnosed in France with only country known, and the dhfr counts in Table 1 combine 3 SNPs into an allele count rather than per-codon frequencies at each site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Collection years listed per location: Bakoumba 2000, Ghana 2003, Uganda 2003, Mali 2002-2003, Congo 1999-2002 (Pointe-Noire Jan-Mar 1999, Brazzaville 2001-2002); France travellers 2004 — all within 3-year windows.

african_field_samples.evidence: 268 isolates are African field/clinical samples, but 173 isolates are imported traveller cases collected in France (marked with footnote a in Table 1); a lab clone FCM29 and a Thai comparator strain are also included.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study genotyping field isolates and microsatellite haplotypes.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Isolates selected from CNRP and MRTC databases previously genotyped/published: 'Isolates from the CNRP repository and from the studies conducted in Congo and Gabon had previously been genotyped' [10,17,23]; samples are subsets of prior collections.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables referenced for the frequency data; data are in Table 1 and Figures 1-2.

assessment.summary: Microsatellite/haplotype study of dhfr triple-mutant (N51I+C59R+S108N) alleles across 11 African countries. Reports allele counts per location in Table 1 with collection years. However, ~173 of 441 isolates are imported traveller cases diagnosed in France (country-only, non-field), samples are drawn from previously-published CNRP/MRTC databases (high duplicate risk), and the dhfr data are reported as combined triple-mutant allele counts rather than per-codon frequencies tied to sub-national sites for all samples.
exclusion_reasons: Large share of isolates (173) are imported traveller cases collected in France with only country-of-infection known — not African field samples with sub-national location
High duplicate risk: isolates reused from previously published CNRP/MRTC datasets and prior Congo/Gabon studies
Sub-country frequency resolution not extractable per-codon for all samples; imported cases lack sub-national location
